titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Gil J. Wolman Papers
titlestmt.author: by Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This finding aid, which is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0., has a MARC record in Yale's ILS with the following bib number: 11411168.

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Friday December 29, 2017 at 14:29

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, writings of others, notebooks, photographs, printed materials, audiovisual materials, and other papers by or relating to Gil J. Wolman. Correspondents include Noel Arnaud, Jean Louis Brau, Henri Chopin, Guy Debord, François Dufrêne, Isidore Isou, Maurice Lemaître, Greil Marcus, Sarenco, and others. Writings include drafts for works including Duhring Duhring , L'Internationale Lettriste , and Wolman Résumé . Writings of others include works by Guy Debord, Isidore Isou, and others. Also includes materials relating to Lettrist films including L'Anti-concept and Trait de bave et d'éternité .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The collection consists of correspondence, writings, writings of others, notebooks, photographs, printed materials, audiovisual materials, and other papers by or relating to Gil J. Wolman. Correspondents include Noel Arnaud, Jean Louis Brau, Henri Chopin, Guy Debord, François Dufrêne, Isidore Isou, Maurice Lemaître, Greil Marcus, Sarenco, and others. Writings include drafts for works including Duhring Duhring , L'Internationale Lettriste , and Wolman Résumé . Writings of others include works by Guy Debord, Isidore Isou, and others. Also includes materials relating to Lettrist films including L'Anti-concept and Trait de bave et d'éternité .

bioghist.head: Gil J. Wolman
bioghist.p: Gil J. Wolman (or Joseph Wolman) (1929-1995), was a French artist whose work spanned painting, poetry, and film. He was closely associated with the Lettrist movement, and was involved with the publication of the Internationale Lettriste.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Gil J. Wolman Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged roughly in the order in which the collection was received.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Charlotte Wolman, Hedy Laure Wolman, and Didier Lecointre-Dominique Drouet on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2012.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Gil J. Wolman Papers. General Collection, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: In-process collection. As of November 2017, this collection is closed to readers temporarily, and will reopen when processing is complete. For more information, please consult the Closed Collections Schedule on the Beinecke Web site, at www.library.yale.edu/beinecke.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes documents and correspondence relating to the arrest of Serge Berna (1952), the Movement for an Imaginist Bauhaus (1956), musical performances, film screenings, and art exhibitions (bulk 1970s-90s). Also includes correspondence and manuscripts by Jean-Louis Brau and François Dufrêne.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original typescripts of "Le Monde du 22 novembre" (105 pp.), "les Inhumations" (20 pp.), "Vivre et Mourir," "Journal jusqu'à l'exclusion," "De moi se dénue une lettre" (52 pp.); invitations and documents relating to art exhibitions and publications (bulk 1980s); and Revue A , nos. 1 and 4, signed by Gil J. Wolman, Jean-Louis Brau, and Eliane Papaï.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the following:
Aubertin, Bernard and Denise correspondence, manuscripts, typescripts, and original artwork for "le sens perdu de l'interruption" (circa 1965)
Berna, Serge: fragments of film/novel, "Les jeux de l'amour et du hasard" (60 pp.), with collages; 17 autographed letters signed to Wolman from prison (1952-53)

Isou, Isidore: manuscript scenario of Traité du bave et d'éternité , with manuscript remarks on recordings of sections (1951); manuscripts, typescripts, and correspondence relating to various manifestos, polemics, and exhibition material (bulk 1961-1963)
Lemaître, Maurice: typescript letters and circulars, some with manuscript annotations (bulk 1962-1966)
Spacagna, Jacques: postcards ad correspondence (circa 1965-71)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a complete suite of 29 plates for L'homme séparé , unbound, unnumbered, and 53 pages of tracing paper with stylized ink portraits in outline, possibly preparatory studies for Duhring Duhring .

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts, typescripts, and original artwork documenting the mégapneumes and interactions among Wolman, Debord, Berna, Brau, and Lemaître (c 1950-1953), with poster for Lettrist recital at the Tabou, advertising performances by Isou, Pomerand, Dufrêne, Brau, Wolman, Lemaître, Berna, and others.

Also contains: correspondence relating to art exhibitions, preparatory work for Duhring Duhring ; a collection of poscards, letters, and mail art, correspondents including: Ralph Rumney, Greil Marcus, Gérard Berréby, Giorgio Gallizio, Villeglé, Denise Aubertin, Piero Simondo, and others (circa 1975-1991).

did.unittitle: Documents and correspondence relating to exhibitions

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscript and typescript drafts and notes, proofs corrected in multiple hands, with production material and documents relating to the premiere and reception, including a manuscript petition against the banning of L'Anti-Concept (1951). Also includes documents and correspondence relating to the re-release of the film, circa 1990.

did.unittitle: Tracts, reviews, publicity mainly relating to Lettrist cinema

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes publicity relating to Lettrist films L'Anti-Concept and Trait de bave et d'éternité (early 1950s); Lettrist tracts and publications, also 1950s, some with manuscript annotations, including L'Internationale Lettriste no. 1; Potlach (various issues), "Toutes ces dames au Salon," "Si vous vous croyez Du Génie," "Construisez vous-mêmes une petite situations sans avenir;" and publicity and correspondence relating to exhibitions (bulk 1980s).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes early theoretical manuscripts by Wolman and Jean-Louis Brau (1950), with Front de la Jeunesse , no. 1 (1950) signed by Tristan Tzara (?); original photographs for the artist's book Haute Ville (1987), with assorted photographs of Wolman with friends, artworks, exhibitions; postcards from Michèle Bernstein, Ralph Rumney, Jacques Spiess, and others; tracts signed by Wolman, Brau, and Dufrêne (1962-64); publicity, revues, and correspondence relating to Wolman's artistic career (bulk 1980s), also including Ou , no. 32, with 3 pp. manuscript annotations by Henri Chopin (1967).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Peinture Dépeinte/Les inhumations (471 pp.); 3 notebooks, circa 52 pp. manuscript, with 30 loose pages, manuscript, including color "calligraphiées" (c. 1993); and unidentified manuscript, 231 pp. (1993).

did.unittitle: Publicity and correspondence, mainly relating to Wolman publications and exhibitions
Les Entrepôts d'Ivry manuscript, typescript, and publication materials

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: Books, Exhibition Catalogs, and Other Material

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes books by Wolman: Fin de Communication (1985), L'homme séparé (no. 39 of 150, signed); exhibition materials relating to Wolman and Raymond Hains, also Le Demi-Siècle Lettriste (Galerie 1900-2000); and an unidentified typescript (22 pp., 1994).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Wolman manuscript adaptation of Albert Camus, L'Etranger (c. 1945); Wolman manuscripts relating to "mégapneumes" (approx. 180 pp., circa 1950-51); Isidore Isou, typescripts with manuscript corrections, relating to the schism of 1952: "Contre l'Internationale Lettriste," "Nouvelles explications sur les scissionistes réactionnaires de l'Internationale;" Touchez pas aux Lettristes (tract), Transit (3rd trimester 1949), with manuscript postcard from François Dufrêne.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes unidentified typescript, 5 pp., with manuscript corrections of Serge Berna; 2 Typescripts, c. 1962: "Sur ma qualité de lettriste: Sur mon inconditionanalité et ses limites," "Le point final d'un ténébreuse affaire;" and Duhring Duhring, early version, typescript (80 pp.).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes miscellaneous manuscripts and typescripts relating to Oeil de Boeuf (1975), circa 75 pp. total; text of L'homme séparé , typescript, 29 pp.; "L'homme est étendu sur la plage," manuscript, 23 pp.; miscellaneous manuscripts relating to Lettrist exhibition (1964) "Séparer/vivre," manuscript, 123 pp., with 3 pp. typescript; notebook relating to various projects, circa 1988, with other miscellaneous drafts, circa 1978-89.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Jean-Louis Brau, 14 letters, with 5 drafts, manuscript and typescript, circa 1950-1964; François Dufrêne, drafts, postcards, and material relating to exhibitions, circa 1965-1980; and Isou, Le traité de bave et de l'éternité , scenario for sound recordings, typescript.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p:
Includes La nuit n'est pas un endroit pour mourir , film scenario, MS notebook, circa 35 pp. (1971); portrait sketches, possibly relating to Duhring Duhring (photocopies, 1979); and
De l'Anticoncept à l'anticoncept, 1950-1990 , typescript marked "version du 22 avril 1990."

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 5 notebooks, circa 150 pp. manuscript; Le livre des mots , complete in 5 volumes, with montages made from photocopies; and Duhring Duhring , typescript, 74 pp., dated October 2, 1976, with manuscript annotations.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 28 letters, manuscript and typescript, from Debord to Wolman, c. 1952-1956; "La provocation lettriste sert toujours à passer le temps," 3 pp. manuscript (published in L'Internationale Lettriste , no. 2); "Circonstances de l'esprit nouveau/La revolution est déjà commence," 2 pp. manuscript; preparatory material for L'Internationale Lettriste ; "On aurait retrouvé la Trace du mystérieux," 1 p. manuscript; manuscript note, 1 pp., on press clipping relating to Le film est déjà commence ; maling list for Potlatch , including addresses of Breton, Cocteau, Queneau, Aragon, Ribemont-Dessaignes, Isou, France-Soir, France Roche, Libération, L'Humanité, etc.; manuscript relating to Hurlements en faveur de Sade , 2 pp.; draft of text on aesthetics, debt to Isou, etc., manuscript 2 pp.; texts for L'Internationale Lettriste , no. 3, 14 pp. typescript, with manuscript annotations by Debord; and dossier with Situationist tracts and related material, including poster for 6th Congress of the SI (yellow, with Marilyn Monroe image, 1962), Nicht Hinauslehnen , poster for Asger Jorn, De la method triolectique (1964), Mutant , Nouveau theater d'opérations dans la culture , Les aventures de la dialectique , La société sans classes a trouvé ses artistes , poster for Jorn, Contre le fonctionalisme , Berichtigung (1961), Manifest (Gruppe Spur), Pas de dialogue avec les suspects, pas de dialogue avec les cons, declaration sur les process contre l'IS en allemagne fédérale (1962)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes original material relating to L'Anti-Concept : original manuscript, 34 pp., scenario for L'Anti-Concept , 67 pp. typescript, 1951, Wolman text on recording of L'Anti-Concept , 3 pp. manuscript, 1951, and Pourquoi les spectateurs hurleront pendant la projection de 'L'Anticoncept , broadside, 1952; manuscripts of early Lettrist poetry, including: Lettrist poems, 3 pp., 1950, Wolman text on mégapneumie, 2 pp., c. 1950, Wolman, theory of mégapneumie, with diagrams, 1953; typescripts of Wolman, including: "J'écris propre," 5 pp.; "Je suis immortel et vivant," 1 p.; L'Internationale Lettriste , no. 2, original typescript mock-up, with manscript annotations, probably by Wolman; various tracts and fliers, including Primo congress mondiale degli artisti liberi , 1956, and Ordre de Boycott , 1956; and André Morain, portrait of Wolman, circa 1968.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes De l'anticoncept à l'Anticoncept , with Inhumations , 60 pp. manuscript, 1992; and 7 dossiers with diverse drafts, untitled, circa 375 pp. manuscript, circa 1953-1982.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes 7 notebooks, circa 460 pp. total, manuscripts.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes typescript relating to Les inhumations and La peinture dépeinte , with publicity and drafts, largely relating to exhibitions, 1970s-1990s.

Documents relating to Wolman's pre-Lettrist activities, including membership in Union de la Jeunesse Juive (1944), reporter status for Combat and other newspapers, student and youth hostel identification cards (1947-1948), also publicity for Tabou .

did.unittitle: Drafts and documents, primarily relating to Les inhumations
Wolman Résumé drafts, documents, and photocopies

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes manuscripts, typescripts, and notes relating to Duhring Duhring , Haute Vie , and Les Inhumations , c. 400pp total; Lettrist tracts and related drafts, correspondence, bulk 1960-62; circulars, protocols, correspondence relating to Lettrist activities of Wolman, Brau, Altman, Spacagna, Isou, Roberdhay, and others, 1961-1962; early Lettrist tracts and publications, 1950-1953.

did.unittitle: Documents relating to exhibition and events
Wolman Résumé drafts, documents, and photocopies
Wolman Résumé drafts, documents, photocopies, and correspondence
Wolman Résumé press clippings and fliers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes early Lettrist documents: cadavres exquis with lettrist poems (8 pp manuscript, c. 1950), Wolman manuscript on Lettrist painting (circa 1950), correspondence relating to Traité de vave e d'éternité (1952), manuscript and typesript relating to dérives and psychogeography, with annotations of Guy Debord (1953-1954), Cannes Film Festival invitation (1952), cinema flier signed by Debord, Wolman, Mension, Brau, Berlé, and others (1953), and Brau typescript with manuscript corrections by Debord; correspondence with Henri Chopin and Noel Arnaud, 1964-1982; transcript of a telephone conversation with Isou (1974); and correspondence with Arnaud, Chopin, Bertini, Dufrêne, Sarenco, Greil Marcus, and others, bulk 1970s/1980s.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondents include Altman, Arnaud, Michele Bernstein, Bertini, Brau, Mension, Pinot-Gallizio, and others.

did.unittitle: Peinture/Depeinte drafts, circa 400 pp. manuscript and typescript
Le Monde du 22 Novembre and Vivre et Mouri manuscript drafts and corrected photocopies
Le Monde du 22 Novembre manuscript drafts
Le Monde du 22 Novembre manuscript drafts, maquette, and annotated photocopies
L'Homme Separé manuscript and typescript drafts, maquette
First editions, journals, and publications by François Dufrêne and Isidore Isou, Poésie Nouvelle La Dictature Lettriste

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the following:
First edition copies of Vivre et mourir (1984); Peinture dépeinte ; Appel à témoins ; Le sens perdu de l'interruption ; and FIAC 81 (double-page spread on Wolman, 1981).
3 test pressings of Poésie physique by Wolman, François Dufrêne, and Jean-Louis Brau (Sept. 23, 1965).
François Dufrêne's La Cantate des mots camés , published by Achele and Wolman, 1983.
"Essai d'histoire comparée du lettrisme" by Isidore Isou (under moniker A. Robin), which mentions Wolman; rare plate of Isidore Isou, S.O.S: Manifeste du Soulèvement de la jeunesse (1950).

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the following:

Exhibition materials, including brochures, exhibition records, and many announcement cards (1973-2007), as well as exhibition catalogs, including Comparaisons (1962) and Third Biennale of Paris (1963).
Flyers, including "Limelight" (1964, two versions), "Seliberté de parole" (May 6-7, 1969), "La Décomposition" (1980)
Le monde , 610 pp. MS by Wolman: "des phrases déchirées dans des des phrases déchirées dans des phrases déchirées dans…"

did.unittitle: Correspondence and related papers from Dominique Meens and Graciela Prieto

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the following: small poster for Jacob ou la persuasion , Galerie Messine (1971); Les inumations peinture dépeinte , Secours populaire français (1995); photocopies of manuscripts (18 pp.), including "Sur le temps qu'il faut"; "Je suis la proie des mots"; "Comment taire le tableau"; "Haute vie"; "Le livre des mots; 8 photocopies of petites baguettes (Scotch Art)?; several small photographic portraits from the artist's pocketbook.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes the following:

Set of six posters: "Attention un artiste peut en cacher un autre"; "Vous voulez vivre mieux c'est facile c'est difficile rayez la mention inutile"; "Vous avez quelque chose à dire"; "Êtes vous sûr de bien voir"; "La peinture doit être fête pour tout le monde et 1 ex avec lettres blanches"; "Vous aurez dix huit ans pourquoi"; two versions of poster L'autre moitié .
International Lettriste , no. 3 (1953).
Tract by Jean-Louis Brau, "Ptotel'asymptotel'asymptotel" (1959).
"Quelques jours en août," MS (5 pp.) with letters restored by hand by Wolman.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes maquette of poster for Valérie Schmidt Sans Typo (yeux + bouches), unique; Prenez un livre dit elle, Bar de l'aventure (1980); Art Scotch 2 , Valérie Schmidt (1966); placard of official gallery listings.

did.unittitle: Posters for The Festival at Fort Boyard
Exhibition Posters, Déchet d'œuvre , Wolman Résumé

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes posters for Calais exhibition (2 versions), Histoire de la peinture , Grand Palais (1979); program (with a picture by Man Ray) for Poète-plasticiens , organized by Liliane Vincy (June 12-14, 1992); test enlargements of Déchet d'œuvre (25 pp., 1981); pages from Wolman Résumé (28 pp.)

did.unittitle: Exhibition posters, silkscreen prints, and lithograph

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes Exhibition posters for 29 Artistes , Essec, Cergy-Pontoise (1974), Espaces Nouveaux , Galerie Spiess, Art Basel (1978), poster designed by Wolman, C'est le mouvement séparatiste qui provoque l'espace , Galerie Weiller (1978), Art Script (1980), Calais exhibition (1983), and CIPM (2007); silkscreen prints "Je ne suis pas fou" (1972?), "Vierge," "Artaud en finir" (1972), "C'était 50 ans" (1979); and lithograph "La vie vraie" (two versions, one signed, 1981).

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
